issues.description: The context object creates circular references by setting Provider and Consumer to point back to the context object itself. While this is a deliberate design pattern in React, it can cause issues with garbage collection and serialization in some environments.
issues.suggested_fix: Consider using a WeakMap or separate provider/consumer objects that don't create strong circular references, though this would require significant refactoring.

issues.description: _currentRenderer and _currentRenderer2 fields are only set in dev mode. While this is appropriate for production, the conditional assignment could be extracted to a separate dev-only module to improve code clarity.
issues.suggested_fix: Move dev-only fields and logic to a separate module or use a build-time code removal tool to keep production code clean.

issues.description: The file imports from shared/ReactSymbols and shared/ReactTypes, creating dependencies on shared modules. While necessary, this tight coupling means changes to these symbols could affect all context usage.
issues.suggested_fix: Consider abstracting symbol definitions behind an API if they need to be changed frequently, though this may be over-engineering for this module.
